reason_not_fls: This video depicts a computer-based number-finding puzzle game with visual navigation challenges. It is not an FLS (Fundamentals of Laparoscopic Surgery) task. FLS tasks involve surgical instrument manipulation, tissue handling, suturing, knot-tying, or precision cutting with laparoscopic graspers and instruments. This content shows a stylized 3D game environment with a probe/cursor finding numbered blocks in sequence—a cognitive/visual search task, not surgical skills training.
video_content_summary: The video shows a trainee or player using a laparoscopic probe to locate and touch numbered blocks (1 through 10) in a specific sequence within a 3D virtual environment. The task is timed (approximately 59 seconds total) and displays progression feedback ('Exercise completed' at the end). This is a spatial navigation and visual attention exercise, not a recognized FLS task.
recommendation: This video cannot be scored using FLS task rubrics. It does not involve any of the five official FLS tasks (Peg Transfer, Pattern Cut, Endoloop, Extracorporeal Suture, or Intracorporeal Suture). If assessment of laparoscopic instrument handling, spatial reasoning, or procedural skill is intended, this game may serve as a supplementary cognitive/navigation training tool but should not be evaluated against FLS standards.
